trials.supervisor_analysis: Stage 1 (baseline_profile) is complete. Baseline established at score 64.2 (11.5ms runtime, 1.42x speedup over 16.3ms baseline) using torch.compile(mode='default'). Profiling identified convolutions at 39.8%, elementwise ops at 24.4%, pooling at 13.3%. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: Writing custom Triton conv2d kernels from scratch is very error-prone and unlikely to beat MIOpen's hand-tuned assembly convolutions. A much higher-leverage first step is to try `torch.compile(mode="default")` on the model in your `generated_kernel.py` — this can fuse ReLU+concat+elementwise ops (which were 24.4% of runtime per your profiling) without risking correctness issues. Get a score improvement from that first, then consider targeted Triton kernels for specific fused operations if needed.
